rag_rules: Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes unless binding to implementation-level subcomponents or connections.
Use only visible ports or constants from the AADL model as variables in AGREE expressions; do not reference AADL property names directly.
generic_rules: Ensure all variables in AGREE clauses are explicitly typed and scoped in the AADL model.
Map requirement intent (assume/guarantee) to the appropriate contract level based on component scope (type vs implementation).
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Wrap AGREE annex content in `annex agree {** ... **};` with valid boolean expressions.
Use explicit helper declarations for range comparisons: `eq range_value : bool = x >= min and x <= max;`
anti_patterns: Do not use AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables.
Do not convert Boolean or enumeration ports into numeric comparisons unless explicitly modeled as scalars.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
